hints: Keep H heads if H≥2; reflip (net value 2−1=1) if H≤1.
solution: With $H\sim\mathrm{Binom}(4,\tfrac12)$: keep $H$ if $H\ge2$, else reflip for net $2-1=1$. So $\mathbb{E}=\big(2\cdot\tfrac6{16}+3\cdot\tfrac4{16}+4\cdot\tfrac1{16}\big)+\tfrac5{16}\cdot1=\tfrac{28}{16}+\tfrac5{16}=\tfrac{33}{16}.$

hints: Only the prime 2 is even; a sum of four primes is even iff 2 is NOT among them (then all four are odd).
solution: Among the first $16$ primes only $2$ is even. The sum of four chosen primes is even iff $2$ is excluded (leaving four odds). So $P=\dfrac{\binom{15}{4}}{\binom{16}{4}}=\dfrac{12}{16}=\dfrac34.$

hints: Use the Fibonacci generating function Σ F_k x^k = x/(1−x−x²).

solution: With $\mathrm{Cov}=(-\tfrac12)(2)(3)=-3$, the portfolio variance is $4+9k^2-6k$. Setting the derivative $18k-6=0$ gives $k=\tfrac13$ shares of $B$.

hints: Imperfections in 10 sq yd ~ Poisson(40); cost = 10·N.
solution: Imperfections over $10$ square yards are $\mathrm{Poisson}(40)$, and cost $=10N$. So mean $=10\cdot40=400$ and variance $=10^2\cdot40=4000$, summing to $4400.$

hints: The put is out-of-the-money (S=35>K=33); long-option theta is negative and most negative near ATM.
Raising S moves it further OTM, so |theta| shrinks (theta becomes less negative).
solution: The put is out-of-the-money and a long option's theta is negative, largest in magnitude near the money. Increasing $S$ pushes the put further OTM, shrinking $|\theta|$ so theta rises toward $0$: the answer is $1.$

hints: Arriving at a uniform time, the wait to the next bus is Uniform(0,10).
solution: A uniformly random arrival makes the wait $\mathrm{Unif}(0,10)$, with mean $\tfrac{0+10}{2}=5$ minutes.

hints: With σ=0 the SDE is the ODE dS/S = μ dt.
The top three sum to 18 only if all three are 6s, i.e. at least three 6s among the 5 dice.
solution: A top-three sum of $18$ requires three $6$s, i.e. at least three $6$s among the five dice: $\sum_{k=3}^{5}\binom5k(\tfrac16)^k(\tfrac56)^{5-k}=\dfrac{250+25+1}{7776}=\dfrac{276}{7776}=\dfrac{23}{648}.$

hints: Of 8 outcomes, 7 have at least one head.
Exactly one head: HTT, THT, TTH — 3 outcomes.
solution: Conditioning on at least one head leaves $7$ equally likely outcomes; exactly one head occurs in $3$ of them, so $P=\tfrac37.$

hints: Only 5s and 6s matter; before the first 6 they are equally likely.
The number of 5s before the first 6 is geometric with mean P(5)/P(6).
solution: Ignoring other faces, each relevant roll is a $5$ or $6$ equally likely. The number of $5$s before the first $6$ has mean $\dfrac{P(5)}{P(6)}=\dfrac{1/6}{1/6}=1.$
